Why movement matters for developing children
Movement is the first language of childhood. Every roll, reach, and step builds the neurological foundation for learning, language, and independence.
Balance activities overview
Beam walk progression: floor tape -> low balance board -> outdoor curb
Single-leg stance: 2s goal for 2-year-olds, 5s for 3-year-olds

Chapter 1: Tummy Time Basics
Tummy time is the foundation of gross motor development. This chapter walks parents through positioning, common resistance patterns, and troubleshooting.
When to start
Start tummy time from day one, in short increments. Aim for total daily minutes matching age in weeks, up to 45 minutes by three months.
Chest-to-chest positioning for infants who resist floor tummy time
Boppy pillow support to reduce arm loading
Rolled towel under the chest for older infants
Troubleshooting common resistance
If baby cries within one minute of being placed prone, try chest-to-chest first, then transition to a towel-supported position on a play mat.
Add photo: chest-to-chest starter position
Add photo: boppy pillow support
Add video: full 5-minute tummy time session
Insurance note: if baby persistently avoids tummy time past 4 months, consider a torticollis screen.
Chapter 2: Rolling and Reaching
Rolling in both directions - back-to-tummy and tummy-to-back - by six months is a typical developmental milestone.
Progressions
Side-lying with support, hand-to-hand play
Segmental rolling with hip cue
Rolling toward a preferred toy
Reaching drills
Reaching activates the shoulder girdle. Set up midline reach targets before crossing midline.
Add photo: side-lying with hand-to-hand play
Add photo: hip cue during segmental roll
For infants with torticollis, prioritize rolling toward the non-preferred side.
Sitting progressions
Prop sitting to independent sitting typically emerges between 5-7 months. Watch for asymmetric sitting patterns.
Prop sitting with arms forward for upright control
Reaching from midline to lateral in sitting
Rotational reach in floor sitting
Home tests
Add photo: propped sitting from behind
Add photo: reaching from sitting
A tucked-chin sitting posture past 8 months warrants a core strength check.
Chapter 4: Fine Motor Play
Fine motor development follows a predictable sequence from raking grasp through pincer grasp to isolated finger use.
Chunky peg boards for pincer grasp practice
Play-Doh for hand strength and bilateral coordination
Beading (large beads) for bilateral use
Bilateral coordination
By 12-15 months, most children can hold an object with one hand while manipulating it with the other. If not, revisit chapter 2 reaching drills.
Add photo: pincer grasp closeup
Add video: bilateral bead threading
Chapter 5: Balance and Coordination
Static and dynamic balance drills for the walking-age toddler.
Single-leg stand progression (assisted → unassisted)
Beam walking on taped line
Obstacle course with pillow crossings
Coordination games
Cross-body activities support cerebellar development.
Add photo: single-leg stand
Add photo: beam walking on taped line
Add video: obstacle course full run
Chapter 6: Sensory Integration
Home strategies for tactile defensiveness, vestibular sensitivities, and proprioceptive input.
Deep pressure input via weighted blanket cuddle time
Textured sensory bins with unstructured play
Vestibular input via slow swings and rolling
Escalation flags
If a child shows persistent avoidance of specific textures past age 3, consult an OT.
Add photo: sensory bin setup
Add video: weighted blanket cuddle time
Sensory work is complementary, not substitutive, to OT.
Research & Beta Feedback
Consolidated feedback from the beta cohort of 38 families as of October 2026.

Chapter 1 - detailed exercise write-ups
Tummy time - stacked pillow prone
Support the infant's chest on a firm rolled towel or nursing pillow for 3-5 minutes, 3 times daily.
Verify Tummy time - stacked pillow prone illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Tummy time - chest-to-chest
Recline on the couch and place infant chest-down on your chest to encourage head lift.
Verify Tummy time - chest-to-chest illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Chin tuck on caregiver's forearm
Cradle the infant along your forearm with slight chin tuck to promote midline head control.
Verify Chin tuck on caregiver's forearm illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Side-lying reach
In side-lying position, encourage reaching across midline with a preferred toy.
Verify Side-lying reach illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Segmental rolling - supine to prone
Rotate lower body first with slight rib guidance to teach separation of shoulder and pelvic girdles.
Verify Segmental rolling - supine to prone illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Chapter 2 - detailed exercise write-ups
Prop sitting with UE support
Place infant in prop-sit and place hands forward on knees or floor for upper-extremity weight-bearing.
Verify Prop sitting with UE support illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Reach from prop sit
From prop-sit, present a toy at midline just out of reach to encourage forward/lateral reach.
Verify Reach from prop sit illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Torticollis - passive head rotation
With infant supine, gently rotate the head away from the preferred side for a 15-second hold, 3 reps per side.
Verify Torticollis - passive head rotation illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Torticollis - active head turn to toy
In supine or prop sit, use a rattle at midline then move slowly to the non-preferred side to encourage active tracking.
Verify Torticollis - active head turn to toy illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Peg board fine motor
Chunky pegs into a wooden board - pincer grasp practice for 12-24 months.
Verify Peg board fine motor illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Chapter 3 - detailed exercise write-ups
Single leg stand assist
Standing at a low surface, encourage single-leg stand hold with hand support, progressing to unassisted.
Verify Single leg stand assist illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Beam walking on low taped line
Walk heel-to-toe on a taped line, arms out for balance.
Verify Beam walking on low taped line illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Sensory bin - dry rice and cups
Unstructured tactile play in a bin of rice with small cups and figures.
Verify Sensory bin - dry rice and cups illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Sensory bin - kinetic sand
Kinetic sand play with molds - alternative for kids with tactile defensiveness to dry textures.
Verify Sensory bin - kinetic sand illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Obstacle course - pillows and foam
Crawl over, around, and through a home obstacle course to build motor planning.
Verify Obstacle course - pillows and foam illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Chapter 4 - detailed exercise write-ups
Two-foot jump landing
Vertical two-foot jump with gentle knee bend on landing.
Verify Two-foot jump landing illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Squat to stand from low surface
From a small step, transition squat-to-stand 8-10 reps.
Verify Squat to stand from low surface illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Bear walk crawl
Bear walk 5-10 feet with straight arms and slight hip flexion.
Verify Bear walk crawl illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Crab walk short distance
Crab walk backward 5 feet - shoulder and core activation.
Verify Crab walk short distance illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Reciprocal creep on hands and knees
Hands and knees creep with reciprocal limb pattern - progressed to over-cushion terrain.
Verify Reciprocal creep on hands and knees illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Chapter 5 - detailed exercise write-ups
Stairs - one step ascending with rail
Practice climbing one step at a time with hand support.
Verify Stairs - one step ascending with rail illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Stairs - descending with rail
Practice step-to descending with hand support on rail.
Verify Stairs - descending with rail illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Standing at low table for play
Encourage standing tolerance at a low table with toys for 3-5 minutes.
Verify Standing at low table for play illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Cruising side-step at furniture
Encourage cruising along low sturdy furniture with reach.
Verify Cruising side-step at furniture illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Squat pickup with weighted object
Squat down to pick up a lightweight ball or block, stand fully.
Verify Squat pickup with weighted object illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Chapter 6 - detailed exercise write-ups
Sit-to-stand from small stool
Practice sit-to-stand from a low stool without hand support if safe.
Verify Sit-to-stand from small stool illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Roll a ball back and forth in sit
Seated on floor, roll a ball back and forth with caregiver for trunk stability and coordination.
Verify Roll a ball back and forth in sit illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Kicking large ball
Standing, kick a large playground ball forward - single leg stance under load.
Verify Kicking large ball illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Sit-up assist for core
With hands held, encourage assisted sit-up from supine, 5-10 reps.
Verify Sit-up assist for core illustration set uploaded to Contentful
Airplane extension in prone
In prone, encourage arms and legs off floor briefly for back extension.
Verify Airplane extension in prone illustration set uploaded to Contentful
